author: @jkrenz77
text: Thanks for this. As a Tx Coordinator, this is very helpful to hear. I come from a retail management background and I’m in a very relaxed environment as far as “selling” treatment plans go. I can see areas where I can grow and get better at explaining the benefits; long/short-term and others, where I can apply my expertise and skills, regarding the customer experience. I’m very hesitant to pressure any patient, yet I can benefit from thinking in these terms more so I feel comfortable suggesting. I don’t need to think of things as “up-selling” if it’s what a patient needs.
videoTitle: Dental Treatment Coordinator Overview and Job Description, Part I | Dental Practice Management Tip!

author: @Crystal3551M
text: I have a question my doctor loves his new patients and we are a somewhat fast paced private practice. Based on that being said our new patient appointments are 1hr 30minutes. That includes X-rays and the doctor to go in and explain treatment. Now some patients have very extensive treatment and we always have an issue with the doctor taking almost 2hr or bit over talking to the patient and being repetitive with the patient. How can we avoid this because patients are waiting over 45minutes to see him and it’s a constant situation or he wants to do same day treatment with the new patient when we already have a committed schedule! 😥 help!
videoTitle: One Thing that Will Make or Break your Case Presentations  |  Dental Practice Management Tip

author: @TailsClock
text: As a leader, this was the last major lesson I had to learn. I was too forgiving on someone because I kept thinking about how their problems affected just me and thought that being patient was the best choice. The damage I let happen lead to a hospitalization... Even in a situation where you can't imagine someone being so inept or cruel that they can actually harm others, they always can find a way. Don't underestimate how much harm a twisted person with ANY power can do. It's been the worst regret of my life that I wanted to be a nice leader more than I cared about being a GOOD leader.
videoTitle: Taking Responsibility as a Leader: Addressing Staff Issues!
